Text._comment: Override any CopilotTextCatalog property here. Changes hot-reload via IOptionsMonitor — no restart needed. Empty / missing entries fall back to the in-code default.
Text._specExtractorExtraGuidance_comment: Hot-reloadable bullet-style additions to the planner prompt — used during the 2026-05-30 quality loop to land prompt clauses without rebuilding. Each line is a bullet the LLM reads as a rule. Append a new line and hit save; the next assessment run picks it up.
Text.SpecExtractorExtraGuidance:
- ★ FILTER VALUES = LITERAL DATA ONLY. The `value` field is the exact string/number/date (e.g. 'West', 'Damascus', 'Paid', 42). NEVER a description ('West Region Ids', 'the customer id'), NEVER a column reference ('[ServiceTypes.Id]', 'Regions.NameEn'). If you don't know the literal, OMIT the filter — partial answer beats a hallucinated filter.
- ★ FOR RELATED-ENTITY NAMES, JOIN + FILTER ON LABEL. To filter by 'Damascus', join to Regions and use {column:'Regions.NameEn', op:'eq', value:'Damascus'} — never put the column reference itself as a value.
- ★ COUNT QUESTIONS = AGGREGATION SPEC, NOT SELECT-COUNT SYNTAX. For 'how many X' / 'count of X' / Arabic 'كم عدد X' / 'عدد X': put COUNT in the spec's `aggregations` array as `{function:'COUNT', column:'*'}`. NEVER write 'Table.Count(*)' or 'Count(*)' as a column reference in `select` — that's invalid SQL syntax. select:[] stays empty; only the aggregation object describes the count. If the question has 'per X' / 'by X' / 'حسب X', set `groupBy` to the label column of X (e.g. 'TicketPriorities.Name') and keep the COUNT aggregation as-is — one count row per group.
  GOOD: {select:[], aggregations:[{function:'COUNT', column:'*', alias:'Count'}], groupBy:['TicketPriorities.Name']}
  BAD:  {select:['Tickets.Count(*)', 'TicketPriorities.Name'], ...}  -- invalid SQL, the LLM tried to inline the count
  BAD:  {select:['Count'], ...}                                       -- bare alias is not a column
Text._specExtractorWorkedExamples_comment: Planner few-shot worked examples for THIS deployment. The in-code default (CopilotTextCatalog.SchemaAgnosticWorkedExamplesDefault) is schema-agnostic placeholders; this override supplies the concrete examples. Pinned byte-for-byte to ShippedWorkedExamplesReference by the ShippedConfig_ReproducesWorkedExamples test. To retarget for a new database, replace this value with examples using your own table/column names.
Text.PreflightWriteIntent: this looks like a write/action request; the copilot is read-only.
Text.PreflightOutOfScope: this looks like a prediction or opinion question; the copilot only answers from the database.
Text.PreflightSecretDisclosure: this looks like a secrets-disclosure request; the copilot will not surface credentials or keys.
Text.IntentOutOfScope: This question is out of scope for the data copilot.
Text.IntentMetadataUnsupported: Metadata questions (about table/column structure) aren't supported in this build yet.
Text.IntentGreeting: Hi — please ask a question about the database.
Text.PlannerFailed: Could not plan a query for that question.
Text.CompilerFailed: Could not compile the plan into SQL.
Text.ValidatorRejected: Generated SQL did not pass validation.
Text.RetryBudgetExhausted: I tried twice but couldn't form a clean plan within the budget. Try rephrasing or simplifying.
Text.RetryDegenerated: I tried twice but couldn't get a query that matches the question's intent (the retry dropped the aggregation). Try rephrasing.
Text.KillSwitchEngaged: the copilot is currently disabled by an admin.
Text.RateLimited: you're sending requests too quickly; please wait a few seconds and try again.
Text.EmptyQuestion: Please send a question in the body.
Text._domainComment: Below: host-specific overrides for the ServiceOpsAI deployment. The defaults baked into CopilotTextCatalog are domain-neutral; this section narrows them to the support-ticket vocabulary so the conversational handler and suggested-prompt fallbacks match what users actually ask about.
Text.ConversationalGreeting: Hi! I'm the support data copilot. Ask me anything about tickets, users, categories, comments, attachments, or AI analyses — I'll translate your question into SQL and run it for you. Try "how many open tickets?" or "top 5 categories by volume".
Text.ConversationalCapabilities:
Here's what I can do:

**📊 Ask data questions in plain English** — "how many tickets are open", "top 5 categories by volume", "average resolution time in hours", "users with no tickets".

**🔍 Find tickets by similarity or keyword** — "tickets about login error", "similar tickets to TCK-2026-00050", "search for printer queue".

**🗂️ Schema metadata** — "what tables do we have", "what columns are in Tickets", "how are Tickets and Users related".

**🧮 Aggregations + grouping** — count, sum, average, min, max, with per-X breakdowns.

**🔗 Multi-step questions** — "find the customer with most tickets, then show their last 5 tickets".

**🛡️ Read-only & safe** — I never modify the database. Write/delete/update requests are refused.

Just ask in your own words — I'll figure out the SQL.
SuggestedPromptPool: Show me tickets created in the last 7 days
